Tests object property name duplication in strict mode.

# Purpose
This code defines a JavaScript object `a` with duplicate property keys. The object `a` is initialized with two properties, both named `a`, with values `1` and `2`. In JavaScript, when an object has duplicate keys, the last key-value pair will overwrite the previous ones. Therefore, the object `a` will have a single property `a` with the value `2`. This code demonstrates the behavior of object property overwriting in JavaScript.
# Global Variables

---
### a
- **Type**: ``object``
- **Description**: An object with duplicate keys, where the key `a` is defined twice with values `1` and `2`. In JavaScript, the last key-value pair will overwrite any previous ones with the same key.
- **Use**: Stores an object with a single key `a` and value `2` due to key duplication.
